channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Albert Korir handed five-year ban after admitting doping
item.description: Kenya’s own Albert Korir, who won the 2021 New York Marathon, has been slapped with a five-year ban after admitting to doping offences, the Athletics Integrity Unit (AIU) confirmed on Monday. The 31-y...

item.title: Titans Claim One-Day Cup After Challenging Season
item.description: The Titans emerged victorious in the One-Day Cup final against the Lions, securing a three-wicket win in the final over. After a challenging season that included losing their first four matches in the...

item.title: Actor Vijay Cancels Chennai Outreach Event Over Security Concerns Ahead Of Tamil Nadu Elections
item.description: In a sudden development ahead of the Tamil Nadu elections, popular actor and political figure Vijay has called off a planned outreach event in Chennai. The decision was reportedly due to concerns over...

item.title: Funke Akindele and Toyin Abraham clash quietly at Iyabo Ojo’s film premiere
item.description: At Iyabo Ojo’s movie premiere for *The Return of Arinzo*, a small but noticeable moment between Funke Akindele and Toyin Abraham has set social media on fire again. In a 20‑second clip that went viral...

item.title: Violent Protest Rocks South Africa Over Disputed Coronation Of Nigerian Man Claimed As King
item.description: Wahala don bust for South Africa as some community groups and traditional leaders for East London protest come turn bloody over the coronation of one Nigerian man, Solomon Ezika, wey claim say him be ...

item.title: Audit exposes cost inflation and procurement flaws in Arusha AFCON Stadium project
item.description: The Controller and Auditor General (CAG), Charles Kichere, has revealed major irregularities in the planning and execution of the Arusha AFCON Stadium construction. In his report presented to Presiden...

item.title: Moi University Launches Local Assembly of 7,000 Computers to Boost Kenya’s Digital Hubs
item.description: Moi University has kicked off a significant tech initiative, assembling 7,000 desktop computers for the Jitume Digital Hubs Project. This programme, driven by MU Technologies—the university’s technolo...

item.title: Hobby Lobby to Open New Store in Former Sears Space in Brunswick
item.description: Hobby Lobby, the Oklahoma-based arts and crafts retailer, has announced plans to open a new store in Brunswick, Maine, later this summer. The upcoming location will occupy part of the former Sears bui...

item.title: Vedanta Moves Supreme Court Against Adani’s Jaypee Takeover Amid Market Downturn
item.description: Mining giant Vedanta Limited has approached the Supreme Court of India to seek a stay on the proposed acquisition of the insolvent Jaypee Group’s assets by Gautam Adani’s Adani Group. Vedanta, headed ...

item.title: Diesel Prices Rise Over P12 per Liter Amid New Fuel Hikes by Oil Companies
item.description: Oil companies in the Philippines are set to implement another round of fuel price increases this week, with diesel experiencing a significant jump of over P12 per liter. Shell and Jetti Petroleum anno...

item.title: JioStar cancels Bangladesh IPL broadcasting rights over payment default and political tensions
item.description: India’s entertainment company JioStar, a joint venture between Reliance Industries and Walt Disney, has cancelled its broadcasting agreements in Bangladesh for the Indian Premier League (IPL) after it...

item.title: A local’s guide to the best experiences in Johannesburg
item.description: Johannesburg, often called Joburg or Jozi, offers a dynamic mix of culture, creativity, and green spaces that make it one of South Africa’s most exciting urban destinations. The city’s iconic landmark...

item.title: Toronto Maple Leafs Part Ways with General Manager Brad Treliving
item.description: Toronto Maple Leaf Sports and Entertainment have officially relieved Brad Treliving of his duties as General Manager of the Toronto Maple Leafs. Treliving, who took over the GM role on May 31, 2023, s...

item.title: Controversial and disturbing moments from reality television that sparked outrage and ethical debate
item.description: The Guardian’s article revisits some of the most disturbing moments in the history of reality television, prompted by a recent scandalous episode of *Married at First Sight Australia*. In the programm...

item.title: Ishaq Dar to Visit China for High-Level Talks on Bilateral and Regional Issues
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Senator Ishaq Dar is set to begin an official visit to China on 31 March, following an invitation from his Chinese counterpart, Wang Yi. According to a state...

item.title: Governor Alex Otti Clarifies Stance On Tinubu’s 2027 Re-election Bid
item.description: Governor Alex Otti of Abia State don clear the air say e no dey in position to go against President Bola Tinubu if him wan contest again for 2027. Otti talk dis matter during him monthly media chat we...

item.title: PNP Reviews Deployment After Fatal Ambush in Maguindanao
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) is conducting an investigation into possible administrative lapses by the provincial police chief of Maguindanao del Sur following a deadly ambush in Barangay Pobl...

item.title: Talia Gibson beats Naomi Osaka in straight sets
item.description: Australian tennis player Talia Gibson has continued her impressive run of form, securing a straight-sets victory over former world number one Naomi Osaka. The win highlights Gibson's growing presence ...

item.title: Iran intensifies internal control amid post-war economic fears
item.description: A month into its war with the United States and Israel, Iran faces mounting economic devastation and is tightening its grip on internal dissent. The government, led by the Revolutionary Guards and the...

item.title: Public Enterprises ATCL and TRC Struggle Financially Despite Government Aid
item.description: According to the 2024/25 report from the Controller and Auditor General (CAG), Tanzania’s public enterprises, particularly Air Tanzania Company Limited (ATCL) and Tanzania Railways Corporation (TRC), ...
